<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Ponytail rules are typically stored in project `.cursor/rules/ponytail.mdc`. I am unsure whether Cursor picks up global rules from `~/.cursor/rules/`.

Personal skills live in `~/.cursor/skills/`. Rules may instead be stored in Cursor's Settings UI or `settings.json`, not in `~/.cursor/rules/`.

The standard approach places ponytail rules inside each project's `.cursor/rules/`. I suspect `~/.cursor/rules/` might work for global user rules, mirroring the `~/.cursor/skills/` pattern. I will check the cursor-guide skill for confirmation.</details></think>

This is the format Cursor standardized on and the one their documentation now leads with.\n...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nyour-project/\n├── .cursor/\n│ └── rules/\n│ ├── general-coding-standards.mdc\n│ ├── react-components.mdc\n│ ├── api-routes.mdc\n│ ├── database-supabase.mdc\n│ ├── testing.mdc\n│ └── security.mdc\n├── src/\n└── package.json\n...\nEach&#96;.mdc&#96; file is version-controlled, independently scoped, and loaded only when relevant. This is the format all new projects should use.\n...\nCursor reads all&#96;.mdc&#96; files recursively from&#96;.cursor/rules/&#96;, so subdirectory organization is for your benefit, not Cursor's. Name files clearly, you will reference them by name when using manual rules.\n...\nFor teams, commit the&#96;.cursor/rules/&#96; directory to your repository. This is one of the most underused practices in collaborative vibe coding: shared rules\n...\n. Every developer on the team\n...\nMigrating from .cursorrules. You do not need to delete your&#96;.cursorrules&#96; file immediately, Cursor still reads it. Migrate incrementally: move sections to&#96;.mdc&#96; files one at a time, verifying behavior as you go, then remove the old file once everything is in the new format.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eHow do I configure a global rules file that gets picked up by Agent mode - Help - Cursor - Community Forum\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://forum.cursor.com/t/how-do-i-configure-a-global-rules-file-that-gets-picked-up-by-agent-mode/157335\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eAgents Window uses a different approach for working directories. Related thread: Cursor settings don’t list or reflect active user rules and plugins\n...\n1. User Rules via the UI: Cursor Settings \u003e Rules \u003e add the rule text. <think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Checking how to set global rules for those repositories. Cursor staff indicates `~/.cursor/rules/` supports global rules in RULE.md format.

Cursor documentation places user rules in settings rather than files. Forum posts suggest `~/.cursor/rules/` may not always work in the Agents Window. _**Agent (default)**_

Both are installed globally under `~/.cursor/`.

### Global skills (`~/.cursor/skills/`)

**Impeccable** ([pbakaus/impeccable](https://github.com/pbakaus/impeccable))
- `impeccable/` — design skill with 23 commands (`/impeccable audit`, `polish`, `critique`, etc.)

**Ponytail** ([DietrichGebert/ponytail](https://github.com/DietrichGebert/ponytail))
- `ponytail/` — lazy senior dev mode
- `ponytail-review/` — review diffs for over-engineering
- `ponytail-audit/` — repo-wide audit
- `ponytail-debt/` — track deferred shortcuts
- `ponytail-gain/` — benchmark scoreboard
- `ponytail-help/` — command reference

### Global rule (`~/.cursor/rules/`)

**Ponytail always-on rule**
- `ponytail.mdc` — `alwaysApply: true`, applies the YAGNI ladder to every session

Impeccable is skill-only for Cursor (no separate rule file). Its 44 detector rules live inside the skill scripts.

### What to do next

1. **Reload Cursor** (or restart) so it picks up the new skills and rule.
2. **Confirm Agent Skills are enabled** — Cursor Settings → Rules → Agent Skills.
3. **Use them:**
   - Ponytail: always active via the rule; invoke skills with `@ponytail`, `@ponytail-review`, etc.
   - Impeccable: invoke with `@impeccable` or `/impeccable audit`, `/impeccable polish`, etc.

### Optional follow-ups

- **Impeccable design hook** (blocks bad UI edits): run `npx impeccable install --providers=cursor --scope=global` in a project when you want that — it sets up per-project hooks pointing at the global skill.
- **Updates:** re-run `npx impeccable update` or pull fresh copies from the repos.
- If the ponytail rule doesn’t show in Settings, it may still load at runtime from `~/.cursor/rules/`; for guaranteed always-on behavior, you can also paste the rule text into **Cursor Settings → Rules → User Rules**.
